name: Hidden valley
summary: Postulates a new confining gauge sector ('hidden valley') connected to the Standard Model only via massive mediators (Z' or messengers) at the TeV scale. Bound states of the hidden sector — light, electrically neutral, possibly long-lived — can be produced at the LHC through the heavy mediator and produce displaced-vertex / lepton-jet / soft-bomb signatures. A natural framework for a wide range of dark-sector and DM phenomenologies.

addresses_puzzles.note: Hidden-valley bound states with mass ~ Lambda_hv (typically GeV-TeV) and stability provide DM candidates; production via SM-portal mediators links DM physics to LHC searches for heavy resonances.

touches_benchmarks.note: Hidden Higgs decays to hidden-sector states (h -> hidden bound state pairs) constrained at BR ~ 10% level by LHC invisible-Higgs searches.
Many hidden-valley realisations include a Z' / dark photon as the mediator; direct target of low-energy beam-dump experiments.

introduces.note: Hidden gauge group, confinement scale, mediator mass and coupling, hidden-matter content and masses.

history: Strassler and Zurek (2006), 'Echoes of a Hidden Valley at Hadron Colliders', Phys. Lett. B 651, 374, arXiv:hep-ph/0604261. Defined a broad framework that subsequent dark-sector models populate; LHC searches for displaced vertices, lepton jets, and emerging jets are active and produce world-leading bounds.

references.citation: Strassler and Zurek, 'Echoes of a Hidden Valley at Hadron Colliders', Phys. Lett. B 651, 374 (2007)
